metadata.seed: Yes. That's something that I get very anxious about, having to cross the bridge. I have to do that both ways.

I have to do that both ways.

patient.query: Yeah, it’s pretty overwhelming. Every time I think about crossing those bridges, I start to feel this tight knot in my stomach. It’s like my heart races, and I can’t shake this feeling that something bad is going to happen. I get this urge to avoid them altogether, but I know that’s not really possible. It just feels so... suffocating.

I can't shake this feeling

patient.query: It really is difficult. When the fear kicks in, it’s like my mind goes into overdrive with all these worst-case scenarios. I start questioning everything—what if I make a mistake, or what if I look foolish? It gets so loud that I can’t even focus on what’s actually happening around me. Instead of thinking about what I want to do, I just fixate on avoiding the things that scare me. It feels like I’m stuck in this loop.

what if I look foolish
